title: `is` deceptively works on literals but fails for equal values built at runtime
rule: Use == for value equality and reserve is for None, True, False, and sentinels. CPython caches small ints (-5..256) and interns literal strings, so is appears to work on literals and small values, yet returns False for equal values produced at runtime such as parsed ints or joined strings. Identity is not equality.
triggers: comparing values with is returns False for equal numbers
two equal strings are not identical with is
is comparison works in the REPL but fails on parsed values
int built from a string is not is-equal to the same int
SyntaxWarning is with a literal did you mean ==
